_meta.purpose: Maps base URLs across deployment environments. The arc nav engine and any future renderer reads from this to construct hrefs. Switching environments means changing one value — not touching HTML or arc data.
_meta.note: Add a new environment block when the site is hosted somewhere new. The 'active' field is informational only — the loader sets window.VEXTREME_BASE_URL at runtime based on hostname detection.

squarespace.name: Squarespace (primary)
squarespace.notes: Primary public-facing site. Formal sharing links. Squarespace controls layout shell, header, footer nav, SEO meta. Content HTML lives in Squarespace code blocks. Logic and styles load from GitHub via jsDelivr.

github_pages.name: GitHub Pages (preservation copy)
github_pages.notes: Full static HTML preservation. Independent of Squarespace. Forkable by anyone. Pages live in /pages/ folder. Same arc logic, same styles — only baseUrl differs. Enable via GitHub repo Settings → Pages → main branch.

local.name: Local development
local.notes: Run: python3 -m http.server 8080 from repo root. Arc nav links will point to localhost. Useful for testing HTML pages and renderer output before pushing.

migrationMap._note: Slug-to-slug mapping between environments. Only needed if a slug changes between environments. If slug is the same everywhere (which is the goal), this stays empty. Add entries here before any forced migration so the redirect logic has a source of truth.
